note: Atomic evidence claims for EMC (source of truth for dist/emc_claims_with_provenance.tsv). Each claim carries: a precise source (citation key + locator), evidence_type, emc_specificity (emc_specific vs extrapolated), fusion_subtype scope, direction, confidence, and any contradicting claim id. Rules enforced: RNA over-expression != dependency; one drug's activity != its nominal target; EWSR1 and TAF15 kept distinct. Negative/contradictory claims are first-class rows.

claims.statement: NR4A3 rearrangement is near-pathognomonic for EMC (~90-98% of cases); NR4A3 is the invariant 3' partner regardless of the 5' gene.
claims.notes: Basis for treating the fusion, not point mutations, as the therapeutic anchor.

claims.statement: EWSR1::NR4A3 is the dominant fusion (~62-79%, ~75% typical); TAF15::NR4A3 ~16-27%; TCF12/FUS/TFG make up most of the rest.
claims.notes: Subtype prevalence; TAF15 is a minority so TAF15-specific evidence is inherently scarcer (single modern model USZ22-EMC2).

claims.statement: TAF15::NR4A3 (and other non-EWSR1 variant fusions) correlate with rhabdoid phenotype and high-grade morphology.
claims.notes: Reason not to pool subtypes; TAF15 may have a distinct transcriptional/response program.

claims.statement: The fusion is a transcriptionally active oncoprotein: EWSR1/NR4A3 binds a response element in the PPARG promoter and transactivates it (gain-of-function).
claims.notes: Gain-of-function, NOT addiction proof. Heterologous reporter caveat.

claims.statement: EMC has a genomically quiet, clonal landscape: the fusion is the shared founding lesion across primary + metastases; no recurrent secondary clinically-actionable driver in metastatic disease.
claims.locator: Trio WGS: SV 53->46->163; Davis 2017: no recurrent driver
claims.notes: Supports fusion/lineage-directed strategy over mutation-by-mutation precision oncology. Late mets can acquire new SVs.

claims.statement: RET expression is elevated in metastatic EMC and folate-receptor expression occurs in a minority.
claims.locator: Davis 2017; reproduced in GSE24369 reprocessing (RET AUC 0.86, C018)
claims.notes: RET elevation now reproduced in an independent dataset (GSE24369 AUC 0.86). Still: RNA elevation is a MARKER of the fusion state, NOT proof of RET dependency (must be perturbationally tested). Do not treat as RET addiction.

claims.statement: In DepMap (24Q4, n=1178 CRISPR lines), NR4A3 is non-essential in dividing cells (mean gene effect +0.023, 0/1178 lines dependent); NR4A1 -0.115 (0.5%), NR4A2 -0.05 (0.3%).
claims.notes: SURROGATE: no EMC line in DepMap. Supports a proliferative-compartment therapeutic window; does NOT test EMC fusion dependency (wild-type NR4A3 != fusion).

claims.statement: FET-fusion sarcomas are addicted to their fusion as a class: FLI1 in Ewing shows gene effect -0.93, 74% of lines dependent (n=27).
claims.notes: ANALOGY only. Raises the prior that EMC depends on its fusion; the shared EWS low-complexity domain drives Ewing addiction, so it supports 'EMC is probably fusion-addicted', NOT 'the NR4A3 effector is essential'.

claims.statement: No direct loss-of-function experiment (RNAi/CRISPR/ASO knockdown of NR4A3 or the fusion with a growth/survival readout) in any human EMC cell line exists in the literature.
claims.notes: EMC fusion dependence is a strong multi-pillar PRIOR, not a demonstrated dependency. The acute-degradation (dTAG) experiment is make-or-break and needs a wet lab.

claims.statement: NR4A3 is germline loss-of-function-constrained in humans (gnomAD pLI ~0.9999, LOEUF 0.37); NR4A2 even more so (pLI 1.0); only NR4A1 is LoF-tolerant.
claims.notes: Developmental/tissue-specific essentiality, not proliferative. 'Dispensable in DepMap therefore safe to degrade' is NOT a valid inference. CNS/NR4A2 is the safety watch-zone.

claims.statement: Myeloid-specific NR4A1<->NR4A3 redundancy: combined Nr4a1/Nr4a3 loss causes AML in mice while single nulls do not.
claims.notes: Makes NR4A1-sparing MANDATORY for any NR4A degrader (avoid the NR4A1+NR4A3 combination).

claims.statement: NR4A3 is an orphan nuclear receptor with no validated selective small-molecule ligand suitable for building a degrader; the EWSR1/TAF15 portion is low-complexity/intrinsically disordered.
claims.notes: Why direct fusion targeting is highest-specificity but currently low-tractability. Governs the in-repo cryptic-pocket degrader program's honest framing.

claims.statement: B7-H3 (CD276) is near-universally expressed across sarcoma lines (mean 5.73 log2TPM+1, 99% of lines) as an EMC surrogate.
claims.notes: SURROGATE. RNA != accessible surface protein; requires EMC tissue/proteomics confirmation before any ADC/CAR program.

claims.statement: PRAME is expressed in 53% of sarcoma lines and HIGH in myxoid-class (7.6) and synovial (7.2); MAGE-A4 (7%) and NY-ESO-1 (5%) are low.
claims.notes: SURROGATE + cell lines silence cancer-testis antigens (CTA reads are lower bounds). Relative order PRAME >> MAGE-A4/NY-ESO-1 is the trustworthy part.

claims.statement: In-silico MHC-I prediction (MHCflurry-2.0) of EWSR1::NR4A3 junction-spanning peptides yields a MODEST predicted binder set: of 34 junction-spanning peptides (all 34 novel; lengths 8-11) across 10 common HLA-I alleles, only 2 are strong binders (%rank<=0.5) and 3 are predicted binders <500nM; best presentation percentile 0.0697. Breakpoint-resolved: 7 in-frame junctions, 26 distinct predicted binders.
claims.notes: PREDICTION only, MHC-I only, NO natural presentation demonstrated. The LOW strong-binder yield is itself informative: per the strategy, poorly-presented common junctions would redirect effort toward fusion-induced LINEAGE antigens. Must be validated by immunopeptidomics/T-cell assays before any claim.

claims.statement: HLA population coverage of the junction candidates is estimable with ancestry stratification (AFND 2020, Wilson CIs, class I+II), not a single global number.
claims.notes: Maintain ancestry-stratified estimates; a peptide useful only in a rare allele has limited reach. Coverage != immunogenicity.

claims.statement: Independent reprocessing of GSE24369 (GPL6244, 6 EMC vs 36 other sarcoma, 23,072 genes, rank-based AUC=P(EMC>other)) reproduces known EMC biology: NMB AUC 1.00 (rank 11), CHRNA6 1.00 (rank 26), SOX9 0.92, RET 0.86, NR4A3 0.82, PPARG 0.75 are all strongly EMC-up; leave-one-out top-50 Jaccard 0.64 (n=6).
claims.notes: A reproduction SUCCESS criterion met: NMB + CHRNA6 (the two most characteristic EMC RNAs), RET (matches Davis 2017), PPARG (matches Filion 2009), NR4A3 all recovered EMC-up from an independent dataset+pipeline. Caveats: tiny n (6 EMC), author-normalized series matrix (not raw-CEL RMA), stroma dilution, single dataset. Fusion partner not in metadata -> no EWSR1-vs-TAF15 here. RNA over-expression is a marker, NOT dependency (RET/PPARG guards C006/C015 still apply).

claims.statement: The antiangiogenic fusion-subtype differential is explicit in the primary sunitinib abstract: all responsive EMC cases expressed EWSR1::NR4A3 while refractory cases carried TAF15::NR4A3; genotype/phenotype analysis supports a response<->EWSR1::NR4A3 correlation.
claims.notes: Load-bearing evidence for strategy Rank 3 (fusion-specific antiangiogenic response). Corroborated in pazopanib (responders EWSR1). Still small N and single group (Stacchiotti/European network); not a growth-rate-adjusted analysis. A biomarker hypothesis, not established practice.

claims.statement: DepMap CRISPR dependency of the validation-panel TARGET genes across 91 sarcoma lines (of 1178): proteasome (PSMB5/1/2) and XPO1 are PAN-ESSENTIAL (95-100% dependent); HDAC3 pan-essential but HDAC1/2/6/8 non-essential; HSP90AA1/AB1 read non-essential singly (paralogue redundancy masks essentiality); BCL2 non-essential while BCL2L1/MCL1 are the real apoptotic dependencies; MDM2 is a TP53-status-gated context dependency; and ALL TKI-target kinases (ALK, KIT, KDR, FLT1/4, PDGFRA/B, RET, FGFR1) are non-essential.
claims.notes: SURROGATE — NO EMC cell line exists in DepMap, so NONE of the following is established for EMC; all are surrogate signals motivating direct EMC-specific testing. (1) proteasome/XPO1/HDAC3 are pan-essential in sarcoma lines -> consistent with the therapeutic window being a pharmacology/exposure question rather than genetic selectivity; (2) no TKI-target kinase shows a broad tumor-cell-autonomous dependency across the surrogate panel -> surrogate negative evidence consistent with (not proof of) a non-tumor-autonomous mechanism; ALK dependency is not supported across the panel but brigatinib's active target in NCC-EMC1-C1 remains unresolved; (3) BCL2 reads non-essential while BCL-xL/MCL1 read more essential -> a hypothesis for comparative BH3-mimetic testing in EMC, with serious BCL-xL/MCL1 toxicity caveats, NOT a conclusion. Self-validated: NR4A3 non-essential, BRD9 synovial-selective. Caveat: single-gene CRISPR masks HSP90/paralogue essentiality; POLR2A did not resolve in this pull.

claims.statement: Extending the junction-antigen computation to the TAF15::NR4A3 subtype (previously EWSR1-only): the TAF15 junction gives a MODEST predicted MHC-I yield (34 novel peptides, 1 strong binder %rank<=0.5, 7 sub-500nM, best percentile 0.30) comparable to EWSR1 (2 strong, 3 sub-500nM, best 0.07). Both share the NR4A3 exon-2 right seam.
claims.notes: Both breakpoints MODELED (junction peptides are breakpoint-sensitive) -> feasibility comparison, not patient-specific. Neither common junction is a strong shared class-I target; per the strategy this is an informative negative that redirects toward fusion-induced LINEAGE antigens (B7-H3/PRAME axis). Class-II (MHCnuggets) + immunopeptidomics still warranted. No natural presentation demonstrated.

claims.statement: MHC class-II binding PREDICTION of the junctions (MHCnuggets, 7 common DRB1 alleles): the EWSR1::NR4A3 junction contains a strong predicted HLA-II binder (QYSQQSSSYGQQPCV, DRB1*07:01, predicted IC50 16.4 nM) plus 12 predicted binder peptide-allele pairs; the TAF15::NR4A3 junction is weaker (0 strong, 2 binders). Predicted binding only -- NOT evidence of processing, presentation, immunogenicity, or CD4 help.
claims.notes: PREDICTION ONLY. A strong predicted HLA-II binding score is NOT evidence that the peptide is naturally processed, presented, immunogenic, or capable of providing CD4 help -- all of which require experimental confirmation (immunopeptidomics + T-cell assays). The EWSR1 junction yields a strong predicted HLA-II binder (QYSQQSSSYGQQPCV/DRB1*07:01); TAF15 is a weak predicted binder. Predicted class-I and class-II results are kept separate from experimentally demonstrated antigens (there are none for EMC). Modeled breakpoint (breakpoint-sensitive). Predictor ran on py3.10+TF2.10+numpy1.23.5 (modern-Keras/numpy2 both break MHCnuggets).

claims.statement: Verbatim FDA-label pharmacology (Cmax / plasma protein binding / half-life / AUC) is compiled for the approved panel compounds (carfilzomib, bortezomib, ixazomib, panobinostat, romidepsin, doxorubicin, venetoclax, pazopanib, sunitinib, brigatinib, selinexor); investigational compounds (entinostat, navitoclax, PU-H71, onalespib, HDM201) have no FDA label.
claims.confidence_note: Quotes are verbatim from FDA labels (not invented); keyword extraction captured some non-PK sentences, so read the quoted PK sections. The achievable UNBOUND concentration must be computed per compound (Cmax x fraction-unbound) and compared to the in-vitro active concentration before selecting panel concentrations.
claims.notes: Fills the atlas exposure gap without fabricating numbers. Most decision-relevant for the PAN-ESSENTIAL proteostasis targets (C020), where the window is exposure-limited: e.g. proteasome inhibitors' short plasma exposure vs the need for sustained tumor engagement is exactly the solid-tumor-window question.

claims.statement: PPARG over-expression in EMC is a direct transcriptional consequence of the fusion; it does not establish whether agonism, antagonism, or neither suppresses the tumor.
claims.notes: Explicit anti-pattern guard: do NOT treat PPARG expression as PPARG addiction. Belongs in a perturbational screen.
